lesson.menu_desc: Second conditional (if + past, would + verb) for imaginary situations -- 31 slides
lesson.subtitle: Aula 16 -- What Would You Do? -- Imagining Different Situations
lesson.title_tag: Professor View -- Sandra Hayasaki | Aula 16 -- What Would You Do?

listenings.text: Let me tell you about a little daydream of mine. If I had more free time, I would learn to play the piano. I have always wanted to, but life is busy. If I were a bit braver, I would take a long trip around South America, just me and a backpack. And if I won the lottery tomorrow, I would not stop working, but I would build a small school for languages in my hometown. Of course, these are only imaginary situations. I do not really have the time or the money right now. But I think it is healthy to imagine. If you never picture a different life, you never change anything. So I like to suppose, to wonder, to ask myself: what would I do if things were different? Sometimes the daydream slowly becomes a real plan.

I want to share what I would do in a few imaginary situations. If I had a free year, I would travel to Japan to see where part of my family comes from. I would not hesitate; I would book the trip right away. If I were younger, maybe I would study a completely new career, but honestly, I have no regrets about my path. And if someone offered me the chance to live abroad for a while, I would take the opportunity, even if it felt a little scary. I am 51, and I still love to imagine new things. I would rather dream big and try than sit still and wonder. If I did not keep dreaming, learning English would feel pointless. But every new word opens a door, so I keep going.

extra_audio.text: First, imagine a situation that is not real right now, like winning the lottery. Then, start your sentence with if and a past verb, to show it is imaginary. Next, say what you would do, using would plus a verb. After that, add a short reason, so your idea is clear. Finally, you can switch the order and put the would part first, if it sounds more natural to you.
